0_Master_Index_Versioning_and_Localizati: Each file carries an implicit version tied to its section numbers. Any edit that inserts/removes/renumbers a section is a **breaking change** for cross-references.

**Rule going forward:** cross-references should cite section *titles* in addition to numbers where practical (already applied in new files 11–15) so a renumber doesn't silently break the pointer — e.g., "file 12 Section 3 (Pain Triage Decision Tree)" instead of just "file 12 Section 3."
When any file is edited such that section numbers shift, every other file's cross-references to it must be greped and updated in the same change — do not defer this.
Recommended (not yet implemented): add a one-line `<!-- schema_version: 1.x -->` HTML comment at the top of each file so a build script can flag mismatches automatically.

Nutrition guidance (file 7) should default to regionally appropriate food framing (home-cooked Indian meals, kirana-available ingredients) per existing product design — this file doesn't restate that logic, just flags that files 7 and 15 must both respect it rather than defaulting to Western food examples.

Referral language (file 12 Section 10) should be deliverable in the user's selected app language without losing the non-diagnostic, non-alarmist tone — flag for translation QA rather than literal machine translation, since urgency/caution phrasing is easy to mistranslate into either too weak or too alarming.
Coach-facing audit trail (file 14) can remain English/technical regardless of client-facing language, since it's an internal admin tool.

No explicit data retention/deletion schedule beyond the one edge case in file 11 Section 8 — a full data-governance file would be needed for actual compliance (India's DPDP Act) before production launch.
No explicit rate-limiting/session-frequency abuse logic (e.g., client trying to log 5 workouts in one day to game adherence scoring).
No multi-language content source-of-truth — assumed English-authored with translation layer, not natively multi-lingual rule content.
File 12's clinical content (Sections 2–6 especially) has not been reviewed by a licensed physician, PT, or CSCS — this remains the single highest-priority pre-launch action item.
11_Assessment_and_Intake_Engine.md: Treating a single missed check-in as full disengagement (requires 2 cycles per rule).
Running true 1RM tests on untrained beginners during intake.
Ignoring pain-vs-limitation distinction in movement screen (pain always routes to file 12, never just "programmed around" silently).
